data.title: Brit 2987 combined ratio deteriorates to 92.3% in 2024
data.description:
Key points:
  • Underwriting result down 13.3% to $141.30 million
  • GWP up 4.48% year on year to $2.86 billion for 2024
  • Direct GWP up 10.4% to $2.07 billion; reinsurance GWP falls 4.7% to $714.66 million

(The Insurer) - Brit Syndicate 2987 has reported a combined ratio of 92.3% for 2024, a 1.5 percentage point deterioration on the prior year.

This was equivalent to an underwriting result of $141.30 million, down 13.3% from the $162.95 million recorded in 2023.

The syndicate's technical result fell to $280.08 million in 2024 from the prior year’s $305.92 million. The non-technical result deteriorated to a loss of $42.45 million compared to 2023’s profit of $9.29 million.

This meant Syndicate 2987’s overall profit for 2024 was $237.63 million, a decrease of 24.6% on the prior year.

Gross written premiums increased by 4.5% year on year to $2.86 billion.

Direct business GWP rose by 10.4% to $2.07 billion, while reinsurance GWP contracted by 4.7% to $714.66 million.

The largest growth was seen in its property insurance line, with GWP rising by 29.4% year on year to $638.42 million.

Financial and professional liability reported $510.59 million in GWP for 2024, an increase of 11.8% on 2023.

On the reinsurance side, property treaty GWP increased by 16.5% to $395.11 million in 2024. However, this growth was more than offset by the 22.2% decrease in casualty treaty GWP to $319.56 million.

The reduction in casualty treaty was primarily driven by long-tail pro rata classes due to adverse premiums development following lower-than-expected signings, with “key clients missing premium targets due to challenging market conditions seen in 2023".

data.title: Scotiabank Previews Friday's Policy Decision by Russia's Central Bank
data.description:
Russia's central bank (BCR) is expected to keep its key rate unchanged at 21% on Friday, said Scotiabank.

Any hope that policy easing could commence in the near term was likely thwarted by a further upward move in inflation, noted the bank.

February's consumer price index moved up to just over 10% year over year and core CPI crossed 9.5% year over year.

data.title: STAR Systems International Granted Patent for Invisity Anti-Tamper Feature for Sticker Transponders
data.description:
SINGAPORE, March 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — STAR Systems International, a leader in automatic vehicle identification solutions, is proud to announce the approval of its latest patent for the cutting-edge Invisity security feature, specifically engineered to make sticker transponders more tamper resistant. Invisity’s patented design will cause a transponder’s antenna to break upon removal while simultaneously allowing for full color high-resolution printing on both sides of the transponder. Invisity ensures superior security while also providing aesthetically pleasing and visually distinctive transponder designs.

As automatic vehicle identification technology continues to play an increasingly integral role in a variety of applications, the demand for reliable and secure systems is more pressing than ever. STAR Systems is continuously innovating and revolutionizing transponder security and integrity to offer the best-in-class solutions. Invisity prevents sticker transponders from being transferred from one vehicle to another and improves transponder authentication without compromising performance, ensuring seamless integration into existing systems.

Invisity is one of multiple security features that STAR Systems offers. Security printing such as invisible UV print and holographic print are also available to enhance physical security. Furthermore, Gen2V2 offers optional embedded cryptographic authentication features which allow transponders and readers to authenticate each other. STAR Systems’ TITAN PRO and TARVOS PRO readers both support Gen2V2 and also support the new Gen2V3 features as well.

“Our commitment to high-performance and innovation drives everything we do,” said Robert Karr, CEO of STAR Systems International. “Invisity features custom printed graphics on both sides, allowing visually appealing designs while maintaining optimal performance and security. The introduction of Invisity reflects our commitment to providing innovative solutions that address critical security challenges faced by industries worldwide.”

For more information about Invisity and other security features that STAR Systems International offers, contact STAR Systems at salesinquiry@star-int.net.

About STAR Systems International 

Founded in 2013, STAR Systems International is a world leader in Automatic Vehicle Identification Technologies. STAR Systems focuses on providing best-in-class transponders, readers and professional consulting services for Smart City Initiatives, including Electronic Tolling (ETC), Congestion/Road Use Charging, Electronic Vehicle Registration (EVR), Express/HOT Lane, Fleet Management, Parking and Secure Access Control applications.

STAR Systems strives to ensure customer success by leveraging the Company’s technical expertise and implementation experience. For more information, please visit www.star-int.net.

data.title: PAXMAN intends to carry out a directed issue
data.description:
Paxman AB (publ) (“PAXMAN” or the “Company”) announces its intention to carry out a directed issue of approximately 1.9 million new shares (the “Share Issue”). The Share Issue will, with deviation from the shareholders preferential rights, be directed to Swedish and international institutional investors. Eiffel Investment Group has declared their intention to subscribe for shares corresponding to approximately SEK 66 million in the Share Issue. PAXMAN has appointed Carnegie Investment Bank AB (publ) (“Carnegie”) to investigate the conditions for carrying out the Share Issue. The subscription price and the number of newly issued shares in the Share Issue will be determined through an accelerated bookbuilding procedure (the “Bookbuilding”) that starts immediately after the publication of this press release.

The Share Issue is intended to be carried out based on the authorisation given by the Annual General Meeting on 23 May 2024 and with deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights. The subscription price and the total number of new shares in the Share Issue will be determined through the Bookbuilding, which will be conducted by Carnegie and which will commence immediately after the publication of this press release. The completion of the Bookbuilding, pricing and allotment of the new shares in the Share Issue is expected to take place before trading commences on Nasdaq First North Growth Market at 09:00 CET on 21 March 2025. The timing of the closing of the Bookbuilding, pricing, and allotment will be resolved by the Company and the Company may at any time resolve to shorten, extend, or suspend as well as refrain, wholly or partially, from carrying out the Share Issue. The Company will announce the outcome of the Share Issue through a press release after the closing of the Bookbuilding.

As announced on March 18, 2025, PAXMAN has made an offer to acquire all shares in Dignitana AB (publ) (“Dignitana”). The offer is a share offer and any costs related to it are expected to be financed by existing cash, i.e. the directed share issue is done on a stand-alone basis from the acquisition.

PAXMAN intends to use the net proceeds to capitalise on growth opportunities and further strengthen its market position, which includes:

Commercialisation of its latest innovation, a device used to prevent chemotherapy induced peripheral neuropathy. PAXMAN will be completing clinical trials, regulatory testing approvals throughout 2025 with a plan to commercialise Q2 2026. Resources will be required to support a capital roll out in the USA, with further organisational development and marketing to support a successful launch;

investment into PAXMAN’s new state of the art facility which will be capable of supporting the growth of the organisation, through improved workflows and additional assembly lines in 2026 and 2027; and

further investments into research and development, specifically topical interventions to support improved efficacy of scalp cooling with new and novel therapies.

Deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights

The Board of Directors of PAXMAN has made an overall assessment and carefully considered the option of raising capital through an issue of new shares with preferential rights for the Company’s shareholders. The Board of Directors has made the assessment that it is more beneficial for the Company and its shareholders to raise additional capital through an issue of new shares with deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights, considering that a directed issue (i) further diversifies and strengthens the Company’s shareholder base with institutional investors, (ii) can be carried out at a lower cost and with less complexity than a rights issue, (iii) takes less time to implement and entails a lower exposure to potential market volatility than a rights issue, and (iv) enables the Company to act swifty on business opportunities. Furthermore, based on the current market climate, a rights issue would likely require significant underwriting commitments from an underwriting syndicate, which would entail additional costs and/or further dilution depending on the type of consideration paid for such underwriting. A rights issue would likely also need to be realised at a lower subscription price given the discount levels of rights issues recently realised in the market.

Considering the above, the Board of Directors’ overall assessment is that the reasons for carrying out a directed issue with sufficient strength outweigh the reasons supporting the general rule that share issues should be carried out with preferential rights for the shareholders, and that a directed issue is the most favourable alternative for the Company to raise capital.

The subscription price in the Share Issue will be determined through the Bookbuilding and it is the Board of Directors assessment that the subscription price therefore will reflect prevailing market conditions and investor demand, and thus being considered to be on market terms.

In consideration of the above, the Board of Directors deems that the Share Issue will be made in accordance with the terms and conditions as well as the limitations of the authorisation to the Board of Directors to resolve on issues of, inter alia, shares, granted by the Annual General Meeting on 23 May 2024.

Lock-ups

Provided that the Board of Directors resolves to carry out the Share Issue, the Board of Directors and such members of the management who hold shares in the Company have undertaken not to sell or otherwise transfer or dispose of shares or other securities in PAXMAN, without the consent of Carnegie, during a period of 180 calendar days following the settlement of the Share Issue. Additionally, the Company will undertake, provided that the Board of Directors resolves to carry out the Share Issue, not to carry out or propose any additional issuances of shares or other securities in the Company, without the consent of Carnegie, for a period of 180 calendar days following the settlement of the Share Issue. The Company’s lock-up undertaking includes an exemption for any consideration shares to be issued in connection with the potential acquisition of Dignitana.

Advisers

Carnegie Investment Bank AB (publ) has been appointed as Sole Global Coordinator and Sole Bookrunner in connection with the Share Issue. Advokatfirman Delphi is legal adviser to the Company in connection with the Share Issue.

Boliden announces its intention to carry out a directed share issue of approximately SEK 3.75 billion
data.description:
Boliden

NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES, THE UNITED KINGDOM, AUSTRALIA, CANADA, HONG KONG, ISRAEL, JAPAN, NEW ZEALAND, SOUTH AFRICA, SWITZERLAND OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE SUCH RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL OR WOULD REQUIRE REGISTRATION OR ANY OTHER MEASURES.

Boliden AB ("Boliden" or the "Company") has chosen to evaluate the conditions for carrying out a primary placing in the form of a directed share issue (the "Share Issue") based on the authorization granted by the extraordinary general meeting on January 23, 2025. The Share Issue will be carried out through an accelerated bookbuilding procedure targeting Swedish and international institutional investors and is expected to raise gross proceeds of approximately SEK 3.75 billion.

 

In accordance with previous communications, Boliden intends to use the net proceeds of the Share Issue to finance the acquisition of Somincor-Sociedade Mineira de Neves-Corvo, S.A. ("Neves-Corvo") and Zinkgruvan Mining Aktiebolag ("Zinkgruvan"). Completion of the acquisition of Neves-Corvo and Zinkgruvan is expected in or around April 2025, subject to regulatory approvals, including merger control filings and approvals and other customary closing conditions.

The subscription price and the number of new shares in the Share Issue will be determined through an accelerated bookbuilding procedure (the "Bookbuild") and will be directed towards Swedish and international institutional investors.

The Bookbuild will commence immediately after publication of this announcement and end before the commencement of trading on Nasdaq Stockholm on March 21, 2025. The timing of closing, pricing and allocation in the Bookbuild are determined at the discretion of the Company and the Bookbuild may be shortened or extended and may be cancelled at any time, meaning the Company may refrain, in part or in full, from carrying out the Share Issue. The Company will announce the outcome of the Share Issue in a subsequent press release after the Bookbuild is completed.

Deviation from shareholders' preferential rights

Prior to the Share Issue, the Board of Directors has made an overall assessment and carefully considered the option to raise capital through a rights issue. The Board of Directors considers that the reasons for deviating from the shareholders' preferential right are (i) to increase the flexibility of the timing of the share issue to minimize dependency on market conditions as a rights issue would take significantly longer to complete and entail a higher exposure to market risks, as well as risk for a potentially adverse effect on the share price, (ii) to benefit from the cost savings associated with the reduced necessity to utilize the bridge loan, (iii) that the share issue, in relation to the Company's market capitalization, is limited in size, which entails that a rights issue process is disproportionately burdensome to carry out, causing costs in the form of time-consuming processes for the Company, (iv) to carry out a directed share issue can be made at lower costs and with less complexity than a rights issue, and (v) to strengthen the Company's shareholder base with Swedish and international institutional investors in order to maintain and enhance the liquidity of the Company's share. Considering the above, the Board of Directors overall assessment is that a directed share issue with deviation from the shareholders' preferential right is the most favorable alternative for the Company and is in the best interest of the Company and its shareholders.

Since the subscription price in the Share Issue will be determined through the Bookbuild procedure, the Board of Directors assesses that the subscription price will reflect current market conditions and demand.

Lock-up undertakings

In the context of the Share Issue, the Company has agreed not to issue additional shares for a period of 180 calendar days after completion of the Share Issue, subject to certain customary exceptions. In addition, all board members elected by the shareholders' meeting, the CEO and the CFO of Boliden have undertaken not to sell or otherwise dispose of their shares in Boliden for a period of 90 calendar days after completion of the Share Issue, subject to certain customary exceptions.

Advisors

BNP PARIBAS and SEB are acting as Joint Global Coordinators and Joint Bookrunners in respect of the Share Issue and RBC Capital Markets is acting as Joint Bookrunner (jointly the "Joint Bookrunners"). Advokatfirman Vinge is acting as Swedish legal counsel and Milbank LLP as international legal counsel to Boliden. Linklaters Advokatbyrå AB & Linklaters LLP is acting as Swedish and international legal counsel to the Joint Bookrunners, in connection with the Share Issue.

data.title: North American Construction Group Kept at Outperform at BMO After Q4 Results; Price Target Cut to C$34.00
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets on Thursday reiterated its outperform rating on the shares of North American Construction Group while trimming its price target to C$34.00 from C$35.00 following the company's fourth-quarter results.

"NOA's Q4/24 results were in line with expectations, while the company's 2025 guidance range of $415-445 million was maintained. We continue to appreciate NOA's inexpensive valuation, and expect recent contract extensions/wins provide greater visibility for 2025 results. Post quarter, we adjust our target price to $34 ($35 prior), which reflects ~3.5x 2026E EV/EBITDA. We reiterate our Outperform rating," analyst John Gibson wrote.

data.title: Walrus Foundation raises $140 million to bolster the decentralized data storage protocol Walrus
data.description:
The web3 data storage infrastructure organization Walrus Foundation raised $140 million from a private token sale.

The venture firm Standard Crypto led the financing, which included additional support from a16z crypto, Electric Capital, Creditcoin, Lvna Capital, Protagonist, Franklin Templeton Digital Assets, Karatage, RW3 Ventures, Comma3 Ventures and The Raptor Group, according to a company release.

The Walrus Foundation oversees the development of the Sui-based decentralized data storage platform Walrus, which lets store items such as images, videos and PDFs. Under the hood, Walrus deploys its utility token WAL to run a delegated proof-of-stake mechanism that operates its storage nodes while preventing Sybil attacks.

The Walrus Foundation plans to use its financing to continue developing Walrus, which is expected to launch on mainnet on March 27.

"This investment is a significant milestone to redefine decentralized storage," Managing Executive Rebecca Simmonds said in a statement. "By leveraging Sui’s unique architecture, we’re making storing data programmable, interactive and secure."

Walrus was initially developed by Mysten Labs, the same creator of the Layer 1 blockchain Sui. The platform's token, WAL, maintains a total supply of 5 billion with a circulating supply of 1.25 billion. Sixty percent of WAL tokens will go to the community, with initial token unlocks scheduled for March, according to Walrus's tokenomics roadmap.

By Zaeem Shoaib

The Colorado Health Facilities Authority plans to issue $148.6 million of bonds to pay for certain costs linked to long-term care facilities operated by Illinois-based not for profit corporation Covenant Living Communities and Services.

The authority will loan the proceeds from the Series 2025A revenue bonds to Covenant, which will use them for the payment of the costs of acquiring, constructing, remodeling, renovating and equipping long-term care facilities, according to documents posted Wednesday on MuniOS.

The proceeds will also be used to refund certain outstanding bonds of the authority.

Interest on the bonds is payable on each June 1 and Dec. 1.

Skokie, Illinois-based Covenant operates a continuing care system of retirement communities, assisted living facilities and skilled nursing homes on behalf of the Board of Benevolence of The Evangelical Covenant Church.

Facilities owned and operated by Covenant's members are in California, Washington, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, Minnesota, Colorado, New Hampshire, Oklahoma, and Michigan.

Fitch Ratings has assigned the Series 2025 Bonds a rating of A-.

Ziegler is serving as the lead manager on the issuance.

Write to Zaeem Shoaib at zaeem.shoaib@wsj.com

data.title: Update: Tigress Financial Raises Price Target on Microsoft to $595 From $550, Maintains Buy Rating
data.description:
(Updated to include Tigress' commentary)

Tigress raised its price target on Microsoft to $595 from $550, citing the company's continued advancements in artificial development development and integration as key drivers of growth across all major business lines.

The brokerage emphasized that the company is at the forefront of the AI revolution, leveraging ChatGPT and expanding AI functionality across its entire product ecosystem. This deep integration is set to accelerate global digital transformation and highlight the stock's investment opportunity, analyst Ivan Feinseth wrote in a Thursday note.

Additionally, the brokerage highlighted the company's strong balance sheet and cash flow, which will continue to support strategic acquisitions, ongoing growth initiatives, and enhanced shareholder returns through dividend increases and share repurchases.

Microsoft has an average rating of buy and mean price target of $504.89, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

notice from copies of the content.

FBN INTERVIEW WITH REP. ROGER WILLIAMS (R-TX)

MARCH 20, 2025

SPEAKERS:

REP. ROGER WILLIAMS (R-TX)

MARIA BARTIROMO, FBN ANCHOR

MEHEK COOKE, ATTORNEY

BARTIROMO: Nearly 2000 illegal migrants on the terrorist watch list were encountered at our borders during Biden's term in four years. My next guest introduced a bill to require screening of all illegal migrants against the terrorist watch list.

Joining me now is Texas Congressman Roger Williams. Congressman, I just can't believe that that's not being done already. So in other words, illegals come in, they're encountered at the border, and we weren't checking the terrorist watch list?

REP. ROGER WILLIAMS (R-TX): Exactly. I mean, these people were just coming across and under Biden, I mean, hundreds and hundreds of basically, terrorists got into our country, and we got legislation now that makes it — going to make it easier on the border patrol, and it's going to be able to let them cross review the terrorist watch list and get these people now, rather than getting in our country, and frankly, not even knowing where they are. It's common sense, but we didn't have much of that out of the Biden administration. That's what President Trump is fixing.

BARTIROMO: That's unbelievable. Texas, of course, on the front lines of this border crisis. For the last several years, nearly 4 million migrant encounters recorded during Joe Biden's term. That takes a financial toll as well. And I know Governor Greg Abbott said that his state spent $11 billion to deal with this over that four year period. I know back in February, you introduced another bill requesting reimbursement for Texas over Operation Lone Star. What are you looking for?

WILLIAMS: We want our money back, and we actually we're asking for $11 billion. We need — Texas needs more than that. But you know, our Governor Abbott and Texas jumped out and did what the federal government should have been doing, defending our borders and help us build these walls. But Texas took the lead on it where $11 billion of taxpayers money and Texans' money. We want our money back, and we didn't loan, that's the thing. We didn't loan the federal government. It wasn't a loan. We just want the money back and put it in our hands so we can defend Texas help, defend America with President Trump.

BARTIROMO: And it's a different story, there today. Isn't there? As — as encounters have plummeted and crossers have — have plummeted under the Trump administration?

WILLIAMS: No, the numbers are unbelievable when you compare the Biden to Trump. I mean, I can't recall the numbers now, but as well, almost getting around 1000 where it was 22,000 under — under Biden.

BARTIROMO: Yeah.

WILLIAMS: So it's working. Stay in Mexico is working. Those kind of things. We just need to shore it up, make it legal. Tell people if you want to come to America, do it right. If not, don't come.

BARTIROMO: Yeah. Tom Homan told me, I think crossings are down 96 percent since the Biden years. I'm with Mehek Cooke, this morning. Go ahead, Mehek. Jump in.

COOKE: Well, I want to get to your role as your Small Business Administrator, Chairman, I really appreciate everything you've done to advance that. One of the most important issues I have is you've called out just like we need rogue judicial activism out. You've called out these rogue administrators that are unelected.

CPSC, Trumka, when you call these administrators out that are using their authority to hurt small businesses, what is your advice to small businesses after you have a finding like that, what can they do to get for instance, I — working with somebody now at the CPSC who has continued to push against a small business that continues to fight against unelected bureaucrats that aren't even going do due process rule making, and so you're seeing a lot of small businesses hurt. What's your advice to them after you have a ruling? What can they do to get back on track, to get on their feet and start elevating their business again?

WILLIAMS: Well, I think they need to make their elected officials aware. We, as they say, we got a new sheriff in town. We got a businessman running the business of America. We got Kelly Loeffler now who is going to be administrator of the SBA, and my role as Chairman of Small Business Committee, oversee the SBA. So it's going to be a really good day for small business to get in business and to stay in business.

But they need to report these issues to their elected official or whatever, because you've got to — you've got a willing ear in administrator Loeffler and a willing ear in me that we understand Main Streets. Look Main Street, it's 75 percent of the payroll. 75 percent of the workforce is Main Street America. 99 percent of the businesses, like yours and mine are small businesses.

BARTIROMO: Right.

WILLIAMS: So they need to report these and not ignore and feel like they're burdened.

BARTIROMO: And of course, those are the real job creators as well. You're also pushing the Small Business Administration to release the Biden era data on reported swing state electioneering. Tell us about this. I know you wrote to Administrator Kelly Loeffler alleging voter registration activities and partisan implementation of an executive order under the Biden Harris SBA. Congressman, what went down here?

WILLIAMS: Well, what went down, I wrote a letter to Administrator Loeffler reminding her of the issues we have with the Biden administration. We had a whistleblower back in August, I think it was who let us know that actually the people in the SBA were being taken out of their offices. They were assigned MOUs of this particular thing with the state of Michigan. They were registering voters. They were not in their office. They were not there to help small business get started, stay in business. They were actually electioneering, trying to register Democratic voters in Michigan.

And we found that through a whistleblower, that's not even the purview for heaven sakes of the SBA. We brought the SBA in. We asked, we — I even had to issue subpoena, Maria. First time subpoena been issued at this committee in almost 20 years, to get facts where these people were at these certain dates, why they were doing this.

And when you bring them in to ask them questions, we had one admit — one chief of staff come in, and we asked him 101 questions, and he gave us 101 I don't knows. So that's what it was like for business under the under the Biden administration. But I want Senator Loeffler to know that we are requiring some things. We want to see travel records. We want to see where these people are, because it's not a purview of the SBA to electioneer. It's purview of the SBA to keep business on Main Street and growing the economy.

BARTIROMO: Well, wasn't this a whole of government operation where Joe Biden had all of his agencies trying to get Democrats to come out and vote for him. We don't know really how that played out and what they were trying to do, but I think it was more than just a small business administration.

WILLIAMS: Oh yeah. No, it totally wasn't. It started with the SBA whistleblowers, and then it developed, and Speaker Johnson asked us to look at the other — the other committees on how they were being used. It was the same thing, but it's kind of started at the SBA. And then it followed through. So they were using people to basically register Democratic voters in the servicing of Americans and the servicing of Main Street and other areas were just being ignored.

BARTIROMO: Yep. Really important point there. Congressman, great to see this morning. Thank you, sir.

WILLIAMS: Always. Thank you, Maria.

END

data.title: UBS Examines Potential HQ Move Unless Switzerland Cuts Capital Demand, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• UBS Group is exploring a potential move of its headquarters if Switzerland sticks to a demand that the bank hold additional capital of $25 billion, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The Swiss bank is intensifying its lobbying efforts, but received with little enthusiasm a suggestion from the head of Swiss financial regulator Finma that the changes could be implemented over a number of years, Bloomberg reports.
  • It is unclear what UBS's alternative base would be, according to Bloomberg.
  • UBS declined to comment to Bloomberg, referring to a previous interview where Chief Executive Sergio Ermotti said the bank would make its case until the last minute.

Full story: https://shorturl.at/jwob2

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1047 ET - Amazon's planned used-car business would likely be more of a threat to third-party listing sites like CarGurus than online marketplace Carvana, Bank of America analysts Michael McGovern and Justin Post say in a report. Amazon Autos, which lets customers browse and buy new Hyundai vehicles, is adding used cars to its repertoire soon, General Manager Fan Jin said on a podcast this week. Amazon's expansion could help drive consumer interest in buying vehicles online. Amazon's model is closer to third-party platforms, while Carvana's broader selection beyond Hyundai models gives it a competitive edge, the analysts say. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

0532 ET - Nvidia shares edge higher premarket after Chief Executive Jensen Huang said in a Financial Times interview that the company would spend hundreds of billions of dollars on chips and other electronics manufactured in the U.S. over the next four years. Nvidia stock closed 1.8% higher at $117.52 Wednesday. Shares are up 1.3% Thursday premarket, touching a high of $119.55. Huang's remarks come as Nvidia hosts a weeklong AI event in San Jose, where the company unveiled a new line of chips called Rubin that are expected to be available in the second half of 2026. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0500 ET - Investors might be too optimistic about Xiaomi's EV business, Morningstar analyst Dan Baker writes in a note. The company's shares look overvalued, with the current P/E ratio at around 52X well above its historical range, Baker says. He thinks current valuations reflect an overly optimistic view of the EV business, they say. Morningstar forecasts five-year average revenue growth of 15% for Xiaomi, and projects its EV business to sell around 570,000 cars per year by 2029. Morningstar estimates the company's EV gross margin to rise to 25% by 2028. It raises its fair value estimate on the stock by 30% to HK$30.00. Shares last closed at HK$56.50.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0424 ET - Tencent's acceleration in its AI expenditure and its multi-model approach is expected drive diversified monetization opportunities, HSBC analysts say in a research note. HSBC raises Tencent's 2025 capital expenditure commitment assumption to CNY90 billion from CNY77 billion in 2024. The analysts note that the company's AI monetization should go beyond cloud to cover a range of scenarios, especially through Tencent's existing ad business and its Weixin ecosystem. HSBC raises its 2025-26 topline estimates for Tencent by 1% on higher ad and cloud estimates, with more conservative overseas game assumptions. The bank maintains a buy call on Tencent and raises its target price to HK$630.00 from HK$610.00. Shares are last at HK$519.50. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0348 ET - Advanced Info Service appears to have an optimistic outlook for revenue growth and mobile handset margins, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart, citing the CFO's comments at a recent meeting. The company's mobile revenue growth is expected to outperform Thailand's GDP growth slightly over next few years partly due to rising prepaid revenue, according to the CFO. The prepaid revenue growth should be supported by personalised packages, including unlimited data and premium subscriptions for video-content applications, the analyst notes. Also, management aims to lift handset margins further by increasing the proportion of retail sales compared with wholesale revenue. The brokerage has a buy rating and a target price of THB312.00 on the shares, which are 1.5% higher at THB270.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0057 ET - Tencent's AI investment strategy should propel long-term structural growth for all its business units, Daiwa analysts say in a research note. Daiwa believes Tencent is best positioned within China's internet sector to benefit from the increasing adoption of AI. Tencent's AI-powered features could drive marketing services revenue up 23% on year in 1Q, the analysts say. As the tech giant allocates more chip capacity to external clients, the analysts expect fintech and business services revenue growth to accelerate to 10% on year in 1Q. Daiwa lifts its 2025-2026 Tencent earnings per share estimate by 4%-5% to reflect a better outlook. Daiwa raises the stock's target to HK$670.00 from HK$565.00 and maintains a buy call. Shares are last at HK$526.00.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2236 ET - Tencent could further expand its user base by introducing AI solutions to various consumer-facing products, Citi analysts say in a research note. As constraints over graphic processing units gradually ease, following its stepped-up capital expenditure investment, Tencent's cloud revenue could accelerate in 2025, the analysts say. Citi raises Tencent's 2025 revenue and earnings estimates by 3.3% and 7.7%, respectively, reflecting the company's latest results. Citi maintains a buy rating on Tencent and raises its target price to HK$681.00 from HK$648.00, citing its solid execution track record, steady gaming profit, and likely rising revenue from its ad and business services, boosted its AI adoption. Shares are last down 2.4% at HK$527.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2211 ET - Tencent could become a dominant AI player in China given its unparalleled breadth and depth of social and user insight, Barclays analysts say in a research note. They note Tencent had a bit of a late start in the AI race, but say it has generally caught up with the rest of China's AI leaders. Tencent's around $5 billion capital expenditure in 4Q should build a foundation for strong cloud and AI growth in 2025, the analysts say. Meanwhile, Tencent's consistent around $20 billion annual shareholder return through dividends and buybacks signals that management and shareholder interests are aligned, the bank says. Barclays raises Tencent ADR target price to US$88.00 from US$66.00. Shares last closed 0.1% higher at US$69.59. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2157 ET - The merger of Axiata Group and Sinar Mas's Indonesian telecom operations should strengthen market position, but integration costs over the next one to two years may weigh on earnings, Kenanga IB analyst Kylie Chan Sze Zan says in a note. Also, the merged XL Axiata and Smartfren entity could face pressure on growth in average revenue per user due to inflation and competition from larger peers, particularly Telkomsel, she reckons. Axiata may face near-term earnings dilution as its stake in Indonesian unit XL drops to 47.9% from 66.5%, she adds. Kenanga cuts Axiata's target price to MYR1.85 from MYR2.05 while maintaining a market perform rating on the stock. Shares are unchanged at MYR1.78.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1355 ET - The EU's warning is "a strong signal to Alphabet that its approach needs to change rapidly," Emmanuel Mounier, secretary general at travel group association EU Travel Tech, says. The European Commission said on Wednesday that Google appears to be favoring its own services--like Google Shopping and hotel booking--over rivals' offerings. Mounier, whose group counts companies like Booking.com and Skyscanner among its members, says the EU is at a "turning point" with its DMA enforcement. "By ending Google Search self-preferencing, it can demonstrate that it can deliver real benefits to all digital businesses, including U.S. ones, as well as European consumers," he says. (edith.hancock@wsj.com)

1345 ET - Telecommunications heavyweight América Móvil plans to pay dividends of 0.52 Mexican pesos a share, while adding MXN10 billion to its share-buyback fund for the April 2025 to April 2026 period. The proposal puts the dividend yield at 3.6% at current prices, plus about 1.1% if the buyback fund is used up, analysts at Banorte say in a note. The implications are neutral for the stock, they say. "The company's outlook remains favorable, back by sustained demand and good growth despite a challenging ecoomic environment in coming quarters." América Móvil shares are down 0.9% on the Mexican stock exchange.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1340 ET - The EU's enforcement of its new tech antitrust rulebook "has been marked by opaque, discretionary process that lacks predictability and proportionality," Daniel Friedlaender, senior vice president and head of tech lobby group CCIA Europe, says. The bloc's competition watchdog moved forward with its scrutiny of Apple and Google under the Digital Markets Act. CCIA counts both Apple and Google among its members, alongside other tech giants like Meta and Amazon, which also have to comply with the law. "The commission's apparent push to impose granular design changes on digital products, impacting the user experience and security, raises questions about whether all the tradeoffs for consumers and business users have been properly considered," he says.(edith.hancock@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1153 ET - European buyout shop CVC is taking a cautious look at 2025 when it comes to investment sales, guiding for exits "at, or slightly above" the EUR13.1 billion reached in 2024. Though exits more than doubled from 2023, they were lumpy and dropped in the second half of the year. Deployments, meanwhile, reached EUR25.6 billion in 2024, up 71% from the year earlier, and were largely driven by a PE recovery as the firm activated in May its flagship EUR26.8 billion Europe/Americas buyout fund. PE deployment reached EUR13.3 billion in 2024, compared with EUR4.2 billion in 2023. (maria.armental@wsj.com; @mjarmental)

1024 ET - Swiss retail clients are increasingly turning to European stocks trading, the CEO of online bank and trading platform Swissquote says in an interview. Its customers trade mostly in Swiss stocks and U.S. stocks--its second largest market for asset trading--but are now looking more at European companies, Marc Buerki says. Swiss customers tend to have a good level of financial knowledge and chase good performance at home and abroad. "They go a little bit where the wind is blowing, so to say, suddenly they see that the European market is quite positive, then they will not hesitate to invest in euro products," the executive says. Buerki also notes that there has been an increase in client numbers at its bank in Luxembourg.(elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0934 ET - A rise in household mortgage debt at the start of the year pushed up the credit liabilities of Canadian households. Statistics Canada data shows credit liabilities were up 0.3% during January to C$3.043 trillion, marking deceleration from 0.4% growth in December. Household mortgage debt rose 0.4%, for annualized growth of 4.8% in January following 4.4% growth the prior month. The central bank in January lowered its policy interest rate a sixth time in a row, taking it to 3%. It cut again last week by a further quarter point. Household non-mortgage loan debt edged down 0.1% in January, following December 0.7% rise. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0627 ET - Banco de Sabadell's investors should have all the information on the conditions the Spanish government might impose on the hostile takeover offer from BBVA before deciding how to vote, its chairman Josep Oliu says at a press conference ahead of its AGM in Barcelona. The offer, which was rebuffed by Sabadell and currently implies a discount, needs just under half of voting rights to pass. While the government can't stop the bid, it can legally block a merger, making Sabadell a BBVA subsidiary if it wins the shareholder vote. Sabadell doesn't expect its retail shareholders--which make up around half of its registry--to tender their shares, and institutional investors are expected to decide once the prospects are published, Oliu says at a press conference ahead of the bank's annual general meeting. But clarity is needed on whether imposed remedies could affect the offer's attractiveness, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0555 ET - Banco de Sabadell's mission is to support Spanish businesses and customers, not to compete with U.S. or European banks, its chairman says at a press conference ahead of its AGM in Barcelona. TSB Bank, the U.K. lender that Sabadell has owned since 2015, is a capital investment which is currently generating good returns, Josep Oliu says. The Spanish lender is trying to fend off a hostile takeover bid from larger peer BBVA, which makes most of its business from Mexico and Turkey. He expects that the offer will be put to Sabadell shareholders for a vote in July or in September. Spain's competition and financial markets regulators are yet to issue a decision after reviewing the proposed merger. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0520 ET - CVC Capital Partners' first set of full-year earnings are a mixed bag, Citi says in a research note. The group's print was strong but its near-term outlook for performance-related earnings is weaker than expected, analysts write. The private equity and investment advisory firm guided for growth this year above last year's but well below its mid-term range. The acceleration of CVC's initiatives in private wealth and insurance are interesting given that the company isn't a first-mover in either channel and it could be hard for investors to quantify incremental upside, they note. "We see upside to guidance and expect CVC to outgrow peers over time." Shares in Amsterdam rise 3.5% to 19.9 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0508 ET - Lloyds Banking Group is set to see faster and more sustainable income growth than NatWest and Barclays, HSBC says in a research note. This should allow the U.K. domestic bank to see the biggest uplift in return on tangible equity, analyst Robin Down writes. The bank's more diversified business model and fee income growth makes it the best placed to benefit as costs and risk-weighed assets inflation remains low, he notes. "Indeed that growth is enough to offset our concerns around motor finance provisions" Down says, referring to U.K.'s regulatory probe on car loans. HSBC lifts the stock rating to buy from hold and its target price to 85 pence from 61 pence. Shares edge up 0.1% to 70.66 pence and are up 29% since the start of the year. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0502 ET - Prudential PLC's results confirm the group's value on a price-to-book basis but also show that it has more work to do to convince investors that new business value growth is compelling, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a research note after the insurance and investment company's 2024 print. New business met consensus for the year after an underwhelming end of the period as it saw a steeper-than-expected slowdown in China, analyst William Hawkins writes. He adds that a lot hangs on its guidance of more than 10% new business profit growth for 2025. Elsewhere, he notes that the dividend beat and the solvency and liquidity figures generally confirm that these aren't binding constraints to growth or the capital management outlook. Shares in London rise 1.3% to 784 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0452 ET - Commerzbank has been "pretty successful" at creating value for its stakeholders and aims to continue on this path as a standalone unit, its CEO says at a London conference. Bettina Orlopp says she hasn't had any conversations with UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel since the ECB approved the increase of the Italian bank's stake in Commerzbank to 29.9%. She has had held investor meetings as is usual to do with the bank's important shareholders around its results and investor day, she says. Orcel has said the bank is comfortable in waiting until 2027 to see how Commerzbank delivers on its targets before assessing its options. "The whole game changes if you go beyond 30%...then the whole procedures are completely different and we are not talking about that," she says, referring to the threshold at which it is mandatory to launch a takeover offer under German regulations. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

2354 ET - ANZ Group replaces National Australia Bank as Morgan Stanley analysts' top pick among Australia's major banks. Both lenders are rated equal-weight by MS analysts, but they highlight ANZ's wider-than-usual discount to peers following its share-price underperformance through 2024. They tell clients in a note that they also prefer ANZ for its differentiated institutional bank, positive momentum in Australian mortgages, good cost discipline and its much-improved risk profile. They have some concerns about ANZ's relatively low provision coverage and limited flexibility on capital management, but raise their target price 7.7% to A$29.30. Shares are up 1.7% at A$29.22. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2352 ET - National Australia Bank's senior-management changes raise the lender's execution risk and prompt Morgan Stanley analysts to lower their rating on the stock to equal-weight from overweight. They also worry about pressure on margins from competition in business banking, lower-than-market growth rates in mortgage lending, and less chance of further capital-management initiatives. They tell clients in a note that the factors will likely weigh on near-term share-price performance. However, NAB still has a solid recent track record on costs and healthy levels of provisions, they add. MS cuts its target price 7.0% to A$34.80. Shares are up 1.2% at A$33.05. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1244 ET - BBVA's hostile takeover bid for smaller peer Sabadell looks like a "shaky" transaction due to the strong negative social reaction in Spain and potential no-merger scenario the suitor is considering, Sabadell's CEO says at a London conference. If a merger doesn't go through, BBVA is envisaging a scenario under which the two separate banks operate under one entity. This structure would make it impossible to achieve the targeted synergies, Sabadell's CEO Cesar Gonzalez Bueno says. The competition authority CNMC, which is currently undertaking a phase 2 review, probably won't take any hard stance on the deal given their methodology, which is based on solving competition issues with behavioral elements, as opposed to structural ones, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)
